toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to run `dotnet test` and collect a crash dump if any test crashes the host process. 

To collect crash dumps on .NET, I can use the `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump` environment variable (or the older `COMPlus_DbgEnableMiniDump`). This tells the .NET runtime to automatically generate a dump when the process crashes.

The relevant environment variables are:
- `DOTNET_DbgEnableMiniDump=1` — enables mini dump collection on crash
- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpType=4` — sets dump type (4 = Full dump, which is most useful for debugging)


- `DOTNET_DbgMiniDumpName=/path/to/dump` — sets the dump file path

Since this project has the Microsoft Testing Platform integration enabled, I can also use the `--crashdump` flag directly with `dotnet test` instead of relying on environment variables.

Let me execute the tests with crash dump collection enabled to capture any diagnostic information.

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-69ccfebea8eb40578e7905a7ee35a89f && dotnet test --crashdump 2>&1", "description": "Run dotnet test with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

toolArgs.description: Run dotnet test with crash dump collection

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run dotnet test with -- separator for crashdump

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run dotnet test with -- separator for crashdump

data.reasoningText:
The `--crashdump` flag isn't being recognized properly. Since this project uses `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`, the `dotnet test` command should pass it through to the testing platform. But it seems it's being passed to MSBuild instead.

Let me try using the `--` separator to pass arguments to the test platform, or use the environment variable approach instead.

Actually, looking at this more carefully, the Microsoft Testing Platform integration with `dotnet test` uses `--` to separate test platform arguments. Let me try:

```
dotnet test -- --crashdump
```

Or I could use the environment variable approach which is more universal:


Setting environment variables to enable mini dump generation with the dotnet test command. I'll try the `--` approach first since that's the proper testing platform way.

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. Here's the command:

```
dotnet test -- --crashdump
```

The `--` separates `dotnet test` arguments from Microsoft Testing Platform arguments. Since your project has `<TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>`, the `--crashdump` flag is forwarded to the test platform, which will automatically collect a crash dump if the test host process crashes.

If you want a full (not mini) dump, use:

```
dotnet test -- --crashdump --crashdump-type Full
```
